questions: CustomEffectEditor.tsx writes to the legacy `effects` table. Should we migrate it to use `useEffectResources` / `useCreateEffectResource` as part of this work, or is that a separate task? (The plan currently adds a TODO comment only.)

For EffectErrorBoundary, should the degraded state (effect fails, clip renders without it) show any visual indicator to the user (e.g., a small warning badge on the clip), or should it be completely silent?

The preview in EffectCreatorPanel currently only shows one effect category at a time (entrance OR continuous OR exit). Should the aligned preview show all three layers like the timeline does, or keep showing just the single category being edited?

success_criteria.criterion: All three existing test files pass: DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx, useEffectResources.test.tsx, VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx
DynamicEffectRegistry exposes subscribe() and getSnapshot() methods compatible with useSyncExternalStore
Registering an effect via registerAsync triggers subscribed listeners
EffectErrorBoundary exists and catches runtime throws from effect components, rendering children unwrapped on error
wrapWithClipEffects wraps each effect (entrance, exit, continuous) in EffectErrorBoundary
EffectLayerSequence wraps its Effect rendering in EffectErrorBoundary
fillDefaultParams utility exists and merges user params over schema defaults
wrapWithClipEffects calls fillDefaultParams before passing params to effect components
DynamicEffectRegistry stores and retrieves parameterSchema alongside effect components
useEffectRegistry passes parameterSchema from EffectResource when registering resource effects
Built-in effects still take priority over dynamic effects with the same name (existing invariant preserved)
Resource effects take priority over legacy DB effects when names collide
EffectCreatorPanel preview uses wrapWithClipEffects or equivalent wrapping pipeline
New files stay under ~150 lines each (EffectErrorBoundary, fillDefaultParams, useRegistryVersion)
No changes to ClipEntrance/ClipExit/ClipContinuous types (params stays Record<string, unknown>)
Manual smoke test: custom effect with runtime throw degrades gracefully in timeline
Manual smoke test: custom effect with parameters renders correctly with default values

assumptions: CustomEffectEditor.tsx migration to useEffectResources is out of scope — we'll add a TODO but not change its save flow, to avoid breaking existing slug-based effects.
EffectErrorBoundary will silently degrade (render children without the effect) rather than showing an error overlay, since the effect wraps visible content.
The preview alignment (step 9) will keep showing only the single category being edited, not all three layers, since the panel edits one effect at a time.
We will not drop the legacy effects table or remove the DB effects code path — just make it clearly secondary with deprecation warnings.
getFallbackValue can be exported from ParameterControls.tsx without breaking its existing consumers.
Draft effects (localStorage) are the lowest priority source — they're for local testing before save and should be overwritten by any persisted source.
structure_warnings: Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.
